body.title: ReaderDevice: embedding equivalence test (same input, both modes)
body.result: 4-test suite verifying summary/nodes cosine > 0.7 via WG sparse embedding; CI-safe (mocked inference + no-DB bag-of-words fallback)
body.description:
Verify that summary mode and node mode cover the same semantic territory for the same input. This is the workbench-testable invariant from the design hypothesis: the two output modes are different shapes of the same content, not different content.

Test: read(uri, format='summary') → embed exec+detail text; read(uri, format='nodes') → embed concatenated node narratives; assert cosine_similarity(summary_vec, nodes_vec) > 0.7. Uses Scraps embed() (T-scraps-embedding-engine, already shipped). Input: a fixed synthetic chunk (no live fetch needed). Embedding: hash-SHA-256-384 fallback (no OpenAI key needed in CI).

**Affected files:** tests/test_reader_equivalence.py (new)
**Design rules:** no live LLM or network calls; use hash fallback embedding from Scraps for CI safety; documents the semantic-equivalence invariant
**Scope boundary:** one test, one assertion; does not test extraction quality; does not test live URIs
**Test plan:** test IS the plan; red if cosine < 0.7 or if either mode returns empty output
